semanticCore: Dry chaff, broken straw, dry grass, crushing, or generous bread breaker
quranicRelevance: Plants drying to scattered chaff (Hasheem - 'hasheeman tathroohu', 'ka-hasheemi al-muhtathiri').

keyDerivations.meaning: Dry chaff, broken straw, crushed vegetation, or dry grass fragments

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيَاحُ (18:45): Then it (the plant) becomes dry chaff scattered by the winds (temporary life cycle comparison).

إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً فَكَانُوا كَهَشِيمِ الْمُحْتَظِرِ (54:31): Indeed, We sent upon them a single shout, and they became like the dry, broken straw of a sheepfold builder.
discardedNoise: Breaking bread into broth (Tharid Hashim) - cultural references.
The Hashim ancestry name of the Prophet's clan.
summaryEn: The root represents crushed, dry vegetation (Hashm), comparing ruined nations to broken straw and earthly life's end to dry chaff scattered by winds.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر كسر الشيء اليابس وهشيم النباتات والزروع الجافة المتكسرة (هشم)، واصفاً زوال الدنيا بالهشيم الطائر، وهلاك ثمود كعشب الحظيرة المفتت.
rawText: الكُرُّ الهَاشِمِىُّ : see كُرٌّ. تَهَشَّمَ 5 It (a plant, or herbage,) became هَشِيم, i. e. dry, and broken in pieces. هَاشِمَةٌ A wound in the head which breaks the bone: see شَجَّةٌ. هَشَمَهُ 1 He crushed it. هَشِيمٌ A plant that is dry, and breaks, or is broken, in pieces. (S, Msb, K.) هَشَّمَهُ 2 , inf. n. تَهْشِيمٌ, He broke it [much, or so crushed it]; (TA;) namely, a dry thing, and anything hollow, such as the head, and the like. (TA in art. شدخ.)
